perturbation.original_text: This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and shall remain in force and effect, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until October 31, 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: This contract shall be effective as of November 1, 2002, and shall remain in force and effect, unless modified as per Exhibit B, until November 1, 2007.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the termination date creates a fixed term for the contract. If Kentucky law requires certain notification periods or procedures for terminating long-term contracts, abruptly shortening the contract term could create a contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: While again not a direct contradiction, this change could run afoul of KRS 355.2-309 if the reduced term effectively terminates the agreement without reasonable notification. A Kentucky court could determine that shortening the contract duration significantly violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, especially if one party relied on the original term for significant investments or operational planning.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-309. Absence of Specific Time Provisions; Notice of Termination. (1) The time for shipment or delivery or any other action under a contract if not provided in this Article or agreed upon shall be a reasonable time. (2) Where the contract provides for successive performances but is indefinite in duration it is valid for a reasonable time but unless otherwise agreed may be terminated at any time by either party. (3) Termination of a contract by one party except on the happening of an agreed event requires that reasonable notification be received by the other party and an agreement dispensing with notification is invalid if its operation would be unconscionable. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The altered text shortens the contract's duration from October 31, 2012, to November 1, 2007. The LLM-generated Law Explanation suggests this could contradict KRS 355.2-309 if it effectively terminates the agreement without reasonable notification, potentially violating the implied covenant of good faith. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing UCC § 2-309, states that termination requires 'reasonable notification' unless dispensed with by agreement, which is invalid if unconscionable. The change introduces an inconsistency regarding the originally agreed-upon termination date and whether the shortened period provides sufficient notice, which falls under the definition of inconsistencies creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Transporter notifies Shipper of such imbalance not later than twelve months after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Transporter notifies Shipper of such imbalance not later than three months after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notification period for imbalances to three months could be problematic if it is deemed insufficient for proper accounting and reconciliation under Kentucky law or relevant FERC regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: 18 CFR § 284.13 requires pipeline companies to provide certain information regarding imbalances. While it does not explicitly define a timeframe for notification, reducing the notification period to three months could be challenged as insufficient for providing 'accurate and timely information' as required by the spirit of the regulation, especially if complex calculations or reconciliations are required. A very short period may prevent the shipper from reasonably verifying the imbalance calculation.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 18 CFR § 284.13 - Reporting requirements for interstate pipelines. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 18 CFR § 284.13 - Reporting requirements for interstate pipelines. CFR prev | next § 284.13 Reporting requirements for interstate pipelines. An interstate pipeline that provides transportation service under subparts B or G of this part must comply with the following reporting requirements. (a) Cross references. The pipeline must comply with the requirements in Part 358, Part 250, and Part 260 of this chapter, where applicable. (b) Reports on firm and interruptible services. An interstate pipeline must post the following information on its Internet web site, and provide the information in downloadable file formats, in conformity with § 284.12 of this part, and must maintain access to that information for a period not less than 90 days from the date of posting. (1) For pipeline firm service and for release transactions under § 284.8 , the pipeline must post with respect to each contract, or revision of a contract for service, the following information no later than the first nomination under a transaction: (i) The full legal name of the shipper, and identification number, of the shipper receiving service under the contract, and the full legal name, and identification number, of the releasing shipper if a capacity release is involved or an indication that the pipeline is the seller of transportation capacity; (ii) The contract number for the shipper receiving service under the contract, and, in addition, for released transactions, the contract number of the releasing shipper's contract; (iii) The rate charged under each contract; (iv) The maximum rate, and for capacity release transactions not subject to a maximum rate, the maximum rate that would be applicable to a comparable sale of pipeline services; (v) The duration of the contract; (vi) The receipt and delivery points and t

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the notification period for imbalances from twelve months to three months. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation suggest this reduction may be insufficient to provide 'accurate and timely information' as potentially required by 18 CFR § 284.13, especially if complex reconciliations are needed. While the scraped snippets do not specify an exact timeframe for imbalance notification, the regulation generally focuses on 'reporting requirements for interstate pipelines', which may encompass the need for a reasonable timeframe for shippers to verify imbalance calculations. A three-month period may be deemed too short.
